title: Apple prepares M7 Ultra chip with 1.5 TB of unified memory for Blackwell-class AI
summary: Rumors indicate that Apple will skip the high-end M6 chips to launch the M7 Ultra in 2028, supporting up to 1.5 TB of memory and performance competitive with NVIDIA Blackwell.

title: Lenovo launches mini PC with Intel Lunar Lake and 16 GB of non-expandable RAM
summary: Lenovo's new ThinkCentre offers Core Ultra 5 and 7 processors with Lunar Lake architecture, starting at $1,179.

title: AMD enables multi-frame generation up to 8x in its experimental drivers
summary: AMD's drivers point to a next evolution of FSR that would multiply the frame rate by eight, surpassing NVIDIA.

title: Valve fixes overheating warning on Steam Machines and raises threshold to 100°C
summary: A BIOS update will fix the problem of the red overheating light that activated prematurely on Steam Machines.

title: Meta removes its generative AI image feature from Instagram photos after controversy
summary: The tool, which generated images based on users' photos, was removed days after its launch due to criticism.

title: Anthropic expands free access to Claude Fable 5 for subscribers amid price war with OpenAI
summary: The access extension until July 19 comes as OpenAI applies pressure with the launch of GPT-5.6 Sol.

title: Lenovo unveils new Legion devices: a laptop with RTX 5070 and a cloud gaming handheld
summary: The company expands its Legion line with a high-end gaming laptop and a handheld device for cloud gaming with support from Tencent START.

title: AI demand shifts toward more efficient and economical models
summary: Companies are migrating to AI systems like GPT-5.6 that offer greater speed at lower cost, while buyers move away from large, expensive models.

title: SpaceX receives authorization to fly Starship again after booster failure in May
summary: The FAA approves the resumption of test flights of the Starship rocket after the accident investigation.

title: SK Hynix shares plummet 15% on Nasdaq debut amid earnings pessimism
summary: The South Korean memory manufacturer falls on the stock market after its IPO in the US amid doubts about semiconductor demand.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of hardware trade secrets
summary: The lawsuit alleges that OpenAI misappropriated confidential information about Apple's chip design.

title: Gamers Nexus reveals a hard drive cartel with price-fixing conspiracy
summary: An investigation exposes an illegal agreement between hard drive manufacturers to fix prices, resulting in a criminal conviction.

title: Google introduces SensorFM, a general intelligence layer for wearable health data
summary: Google's new tool converts the noisy stream of data from wearable sensors into useful information for health tracking.

title: Anthropic's Claude Code now includes an integrated browser to read and interact with websites
summary: The new update allows the AI to navigate external web pages, read content, and click autonomously.

title: TSMC expands its advanced packaging plants in Chiayi, Taiwan
summary: The world's largest foundry invests in new packaging facilities to meet demand for advanced chips.

title: Pixel 11 Pro Fold design leaks with renewed "Pine" camera module
summary: Leaked images show a redesign of Google's foldable phone with a revamped camera module.

title: Uber intensifies its lobbying in the robotaxi sector, clashing with Waymo
summary: Uber's pressure to boost its autonomous taxi service creates tensions with Waymo, the market leader.

title: Meta expands its plans for the Hyperion supercluster to 5 GW and invests $1 billion in infrastructure in Louisiana
summary: Meta's AI project reaches a capacity of 5 GW, with a total planned investment of over $50 billion.

title: Data centers in Ireland consumed nearly as much electricity as all households in the country in 2025
summary: A report reveals that servers accounted for 23% of national electricity consumption, despite restrictions on connections.

title: German AI consortium publishes Soofi S, a 30B open model leading benchmarks in English and German
summary: The Soofi S model outperforms other open models in performance tests in both languages, boosting AI research.
